questions.question: You open the fridge and something smells off. You:

options.text: Close it and pretend it's not your problem
Smell it again just to confirm how bad it is
Check every item systematically
Ask someone else to smell it first

questions.question: Which of these is genuinely the worst physical feeling?

options.text: Wet socks squishing in your shoes
Biting the inside of your cheek while eating
A mosquito bite you absolutely cannot scratch
Stepping on a Lego in the dark

questions.question: Pick the smell that hits you the hardest emotionally:

options.text: Rain hitting hot concrete in summer
The inside of an old paperback book
Sunscreen on a beach day
A specific food from your childhood kitchen

questions.question: There's a mosquito somewhere in your dark room. You:

options.text: Hunt it with the lights on for 20 minutes minimum
Pull the covers entirely over your head
Give up, grab your phone, and accept your fate
Fall asleep anyway — you're too tired to care

questions.question: The shower suddenly goes ice cold. Your first move is:

options.text: A sound comes out of you that cannot be described
You freeze completely and stop breathing
You leap out immediately without thinking
You calmly adjust the temperature dial

questions.question: You bite into food and hit a completely wrong texture. You:

options.text: Stop eating entirely — appetite gone forever
Spit it out without hesitation or shame
Cautiously investigate what it actually was
Swallow it and try very hard to forget

questions.question: Someone waves at you in public. You wave back. They meant someone behind you. You:

options.text: Slowly lower your hand and stare at the floor
Pretend you were stretching your arm out
Wave even more confidently to commit to the bit
Make direct eye contact and nod like nothing happened

questions.question: The waiter says 'enjoy your meal' and you say 'you too.' You:

options.text: Think about it randomly for the next three days
Whisper 'I meant thank you' to absolutely no one
Laugh it off immediately and move on
It genuinely doesn't bother you at all

questions.question: You say something in a group and nobody reacts. You:

options.text: Repeat it slightly louder to give it another shot
Pretend you never said anything at all
Laugh quietly at yourself and let it go
Ask 'did nobody hear that?' out loud

questions.question: You and a stranger keep dodging the same direction in a hallway. You:

options.text: Do an awkward little dance move to break the tension
Stop completely and gesture dramatically for them to go
Stare at your phone like you suddenly got an urgent text
Barrel forward and let them figure it out

questions.question: You accidentally called your teacher 'mom' once. How long did it live in your head?

options.text: It still lives there. Right now. Always.
A few weeks of random cringe flashbacks
A couple of days, then I moved on
I genuinely forgot about it almost immediately

questions.question: Someone is walking behind you and you're unsure whether to hold the door. You:

options.text: Hold it from an uncomfortably far distance and regret it
Speed-walk so the gap makes the decision easier
Hold it normally like a relaxed functioning human
Don't even think about it — just walk through

questions.question: Your phone hits 3% battery and you have no charger. You:

options.text: Panic immediately and tell everyone nearby
Enable low power mode and ration it like a survivalist
Use it normally — whatever happens, happens
Turn it off completely to 'save' it for later

questions.question: The leftovers are probably bad. You:

options.text: Smell them anyway — you need to know
Eat them anyway — you're hungry and optimistic
Throw them without even opening the container
Hand them to someone else to smell first

questions.question: You're in bed and realize the lights are still on. You:

options.text: Lay there negotiating with yourself for at least 5 minutes
Get up immediately without hesitation
Accept the light and sleep through it anyway
This scenario has literally never happened to you

questions.question: You have something important tomorrow. Tonight you:

options.text: Cannot sleep because your brain absolutely refuses
Sleep fine but set six alarms just to be safe
Stay up too late anyway and regret it tomorrow
Sleep perfectly like a calm, functioning adult

questions.question: You've read the same paragraph three times and absorbed nothing. You:

options.text: Read it a fourth time with intense, misplaced confidence
Give up and stare at the wall for a while
Skip it and hope it wasn't important
Put it down entirely and do something else

questions.question: A notification pops up while you're in the middle of something. You:

options.text: Check it immediately — it might be important
Try to ignore it, fail about 30 seconds later
Check it, get distracted, forget what you were doing
Ignore it completely without any effort at all

questions.question: The most dangerous playground equipment from your childhood was:

options.text: The metal slide that burned your legs in summer heat
The merry-go-round that spun genuinely too fast
The monkey bars hanging directly over concrete
The seesaw that left you stranded helplessly in the air

questions.question: When the teacher announced a movie day, you felt:

options.text: Instant, pure, unfiltered joy
Suspicious — what's the actual catch here
Relieved that you didn't have to think today
Completely indifferent — movies weren't special

questions.question: Waiting for a slow computer to load felt like:

options.text: A personal attack directed specifically at you
A chance to go do something else and come back
Pure concentrated rage building slowly inside you
Totally fine — patience has always been your thing

questions.question: On a long childhood car trip, you mostly:

options.text: Stared out the window and got deeply philosophical
Asked 'are we there yet' more times than anyone admits
Played license plate games or I-spy until it stopped being fun
Slept the entire time without issue

questions.question: Hearing ice cream truck music in the distance as a kid meant:

options.text: Drop everything — this is a time-sensitive emergency
Excitement immediately followed by realizing you had no money
Pure summer captured in a single sound
Background noise — it didn't mean much to you

questions.question: When the internet went out at home, you:

options.text: Grabbed your phone to Google why the internet was down
Reset the router at least four times before accepting it
Immediately blamed everyone else in the house
Accepted it peacefully and found something else to do

questions.question: It is 2am. You are not hungry. You:

options.text: Go to the kitchen anyway — just to look around
Stare at the ceiling thinking specifically about food
Eat something you will absolutely regret in the morning
Sleep like a normal, well-adjusted human being

questions.question: Your ideal superpower would honestly be:

options.text: Never needing sleep ever again
Always finding a parking spot immediately
Food never going bad in your fridge
Flying through the sky like a superhero

questions.question: You drop your phone face-down. Before picking it up you:

options.text: Brace yourself emotionally for the absolute worst
Leave it there for a moment just to cope with the fear
Pick it up immediately — no point in waiting
Close your eyes before flipping it over

questions.question: Someone cancels plans you were secretly dreading. You feel:

options.text: Pure, unfiltered, almost spiritual relief
Relieved but you send a convincing 'nooo that's such a shame' text
You'd already mentally cancelled in your head anyway
Genuinely disappointed — you were actually looking forward to it

questions.question: Your villain origin story would most likely involve:

options.text: Slow walkers blocking the entire pavement side by side
Someone eating loudly right next to you
A printer failing at the single most critical moment
People on speakerphone in completely public spaces

questions.question: You hear your name mentioned in a conversation across the room. You:

options.text: Immediately strain every sense to hear the rest of it
Make intense accidental eye contact with whoever said it
Pretend you heard absolutely nothing and act normal
Your name? You heard nothing. You were focused.
